observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
Subject to availability of funds, the LCE consider the assessment and prioritization of the training needs of the members of the Inventory and Disposal Committee on disposal of unserviceable properties; and
The Inventory and Disposal Committee initiate the actual disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations. After such disposal, effect the necessary adjustments in the books to ensure the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the FS.
We recommended that the Management revisit the computation of the accountabilities of the AO and increase the fidelity bond, when warranted.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accounting Office and MEO submit any available documents to support the recognition of the loss on assets;
Prospectively, the LCE, in coordination with the concerned offices, ensure that Requests for Relief from Accountability for property loss due to fortuitous events or natural calamities will be filed with the COA together with the required documents prescribed by COA Memorandum No. 92-751 in order for such request to be given due course.
Pending the COA decision on such Requests for Relief from Accountability, the details of the loss shall be disclosed in the Notes to FS.

We recommended that:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer, using existing, available, and reliable alternative documents/records, issue ICSs and PARs to the end-users who received the items below the capitalization threshold but with serviceable life of more than one year and items recognized as PPE, respectively, that were acquired and issued by the LGU over the years in order to re-establish accountability over these properties; and
Prospectively, the General Services Officer secure and maintain complete files of duly signed ICSs and PARs in connection with the issuances of items below the capitalization threshold but with serviceable life of more than one year and items recognized as PPE, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Reconcile with Municipal Accountant on the noted variance; and
Conduct intensive tax campaign, employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 in order to persuade the Taxpayers to pay their tax on time.

We recommended that the Management, through LDRRMO, ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the LDRRMF through implementation of programs and projects designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as projects pertaining to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accounting Office take necessary action enumerated in Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, and evaluate the existence and collectability of the accounts; and
If all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers have been exhausted and documented but failed to collect, then proceed in procedures enumerated in Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; prepare a request for authority to write-off governed by the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the COA (RRPC) and Sections 9.0 and 10.0 of the same Circular.

We recommended that Municipal Accounting Office strictly adhere with the proper classification of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and ensure the uniformity of GL and FS balances as at year-end.

We recommended that the:
BAC strictly comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on procurement;
BAC fast track the submission of the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation of the Audit Team, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, when warranted; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that payment should be made only for claims supported with complete documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Provide the Auditor every beginning of the year the required list of all on-going PPAs, as well as the list of PPAs to be implemented during the year; and
Inform the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Consider the equitable distribution of PPAs listed among the three important aspects of sector development mandated under DILG/DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and previous issuances as well as the requirements of Sections 16 and 17 of the LGC of 1991, in order to cater not only the social development needs of the constituents but also livelihood, entrepreneurship/ local economic development PPAs which will directly address the actual needs of the intended beneficiaries; and
Adopt a well-defined strategy in order to enhance the present monitoring and review activities during the planning, procurement, contract implementation and program evaluation phase, in order for the planners, implementers as well as evaluators to continuously monitor the timely implementation, so that immediate appropriate and corrective actions could be done on noted gaps along the way.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Market Administrator to intensify collection activities against these delinquent space/stall holders and real property owners, giving them final grace period after which to initiate appropriate sanctions based on their lease contract agreements and available legal remedies in order to enforce compliance;

Coordinate and devise a comprehensive strategy primarily focused on the collection of delinquent property taxes to substantially reduce the outstanding amount of delinquencies including but not limited to the following:
Posting of the List of Delinquent Taxpayers to each of the 19 Barangays of the Municipality;
Endorsement to the Provincial Treasury Office long outstanding accounts with large balances for the issuance of Warrant of Levy; and
Request additional plantilla position to facilitate effective property tax collection.
